# ADR-260007: Legal-content rollout strategy and visual-direction finalization

Status: Accepted
Date: 2026-05-21
Owners: Repository owner

## Context

All prior sections are decided except two section-9 open questions: how legal pages should be
handled for launch, and which visual direction the new public site should adopt relative to the
archive.

## Decision

- Legal pages rollout uses a phased policy:
  - Phase 1 (launch): carry over legal/support content from archive after accuracy review and
    light cleanup.
  - Phase 2 (post-launch): complete a full legal/copy rewrite after product/legal wording is
    validated.
- Visual direction is a modernized, brand-preserving refresh:
  - keep brand identity anchors (logo and valid core brand cues),
  - The visual direction will follow a modernized, brand-preserving refresh that establishes a new design system for layout, typography, and components.

## Alternatives Considered

- Full legal rewrite before launch: Rejected because it delays publication without proportional risk
  reduction if reviewed carry-over content is available.
- Publish archive legal/support copy unreviewed: Rejected due to accuracy/compliance risk.
- Full visual clone of archive: Rejected because it imports outdated visual and implementation-era
  decisions.
- Full visual reinvention with no brand continuity: Rejected because it weakens recognition and
  product continuity.

## Consequences

- Positive: Enables timely launch with compliant baseline legal/support coverage.
- Positive: Balances brand continuity with modernized public-site presentation.
- Negative: Requires two-phase legal copy management and follow-up rewrite work.
- Risk: If phase-2 rewrite is not scheduled, phase-1 legal text may remain longer than intended.

## Verification

- Phase-1 legal/support content is reviewed and published before release sign-off.
- A tracked backlog item exists for phase-2 legal rewrite completion.
- UI implementation follows the modernized design direction and establishes new layout patterns.

## Rollback

If reviewed legal text is found inaccurate after release, replace or unpublish affected sections
immediately and prioritize corrective update. If visual direction drifts, realign via follow-up
design pass tied to this ADR.
